name: Kalif Raymond
headline: Efficient depth piece who needs a route to volume
body:
Raymond's 2025 line tells the story of a high-floor role player rather than a fantasy asset. On just 36.6% of snaps he ran 214 routes, drew 30 targets and a 5.5% target share, and turned them into 24 grabs for 289 yards and a score. The underlying marks stay sharp: a 0.507 EPA per play, a 66.7% success rate, an 80% catch rate, and zero drops, with 1.35 yards per route run. That efficiency is real, but it never scales because the volume isn't there.

Now 31 and on a fresh deal in Chicago, he profiles as a depth receiver buried behind the Bears' top options, with a draft cost deep past WR100 reflecting that. His value remains rooted in special teams and spot snaps. Unless an injury opens meaningful route share, treat him as a name to monitor rather than draft in any standard format.
